title: Vehicle flush window system, a vehicle comprising same, and related assembly method
abstract: A vehicle flush window system includes a slider member (40), a guide structure (48), a glass run seal (70), and a capping carrier (60). The slider member is secured to an interior surface (15b) of a vehicle door window (15). The guiding structure is mountable on the vehicle door. The guiding structure (48) includes a receiving bracket (50) having a guide channel (54) configured to slidingly receive the slider member (40). The glass run seal (70) is interposed between the receiving bracket (50) and the slider member (40) in the guide channel (54). The capping carrier (60) is retains the seal, and mounts a cover (58) in flush relation to an exterior surface of the door window (15), when the interior surface (15b) of the window is secured to the slider member (40) and the slider member is slidingly received in the guide channel (54) in a window closed position.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A vehicle flush window system for a vehicle door including a door window having an exterior surface and an opposite interior surface, the vehicle flush window system comprising: a slider member configured to be secured to the interior surface of the door window; and a guiding structure mountable on the vehicle door, the guiding structure comprising a receiving bracket having a guide channel configured to slidingly receive at least part of the slider member, a glass run seal at least a portion of which is configured to be interposed between the receiving bracket and the slider member when the at least part of the slider member is slidingly received in the guide channel, and a capping carrier configured to lock the glass run seal in contact with the slider member when the interior surface of the door window is secured to the slider member and the at least part of the slider member is slidingly received in the guide channel. 2. The vehicle flush window system of claim 1, wherein the capping carrier is further configured to secure a cover in flush relation to the exterior surface of the door window when the interior surface of the door window is secured to the slider member and the at least part of the slider member is slidingly received in the guide channel. 3. The vehicle flush window system of claim 1, wherein the glass run seal is made of a thermoplastic elastomer. 4. The vehicle flush window system of claim 1, wherein the capping carrier comprises an inner carrier leg, and wherein the capping carrier is configured to be secured to the receiving bracket so that the inner carrier leg extends into the guide channel between the glass run seal and the receiving bracket. 5. The vehicle flush window system of claim 1, wherein the glass run seal comprises an articulating portion. 6. The vehicle flush window system of claim 5, wherein the capping carrier comprises an outer carrier leg configured to lock the articulating portion in contact with the slider member. 7. The vehicle flush window system of claim 5, wherein the capping carrier is configured to lock the articulating portion in contact with the slider member. 8. The vehicle flush window system of claim 1, wherein the slider member comprises y-axis biasing members contacting the glass run seal. 9. The vehicle flush window system of claim 8, wherein the slider member comprises x-axis biasing members contacting the glass run seal. 10. The vehicle flush window system of claim 1, wherein the slider member and the glass run seal face the interior surface of the door window but are confined not to extend beyond 

 an edge of the door window when the interior surface of the door window is secured to the slider member and the at least part of the slider member is slidingly received in the guide channel. 11. A vehicle flush window system for a vehicle door including a door window having an exterior surface and an opposite interior surface, the vehicle flush window system comprising: a slider member configured to be secured to the interior surface of the door window; and a guiding structure mountable on the vehicle door, the guiding structure comprising a receiving bracket having a guide channel configured to slidingly receive at least part of the slider member, a glass run seal at least portion of which is configured to be interposed between the receiving bracket and the slider member when the at least part of the slider member is slidingly received in the guide channel, and a capping carrier configured to secure a cover in flush relation to the exterior surface of the door window when the interior surface of the door window is secured to the slider member and the at least part of the slider member is slidingly received in the guide channel. 12. The vehicle flush window system of claim 11, wherein the glass run seal is made of a thermoplastic elastomer. 13. The vehicle flush window system of claim 11, wherein the capping carrier comprises an inner carrier leg, and wherein the capping carrier is configured to be secured to the receiving bracket so that the inner carrier leg extends into the guide channel between the glass run seal and the receiving bracket. 14. The vehicle flush window system of claim 11, wherein the glass run seal comprises an articulating portion. 15. The vehicle flush window system of claim 14, wherein the capping carrier comprises an outer carrier leg configured to lock the articulating portion in contact with the slider member. 16. The vehicle flush window system of claim 14, wherein the capping carrier is configured to lock the articulating portion in contact with the slider member. 17. The vehicle flush window system of claim 11, wherein the slider member comprises y-axis biasing members contacting the glass run seal. 18. The vehicle flush window system of claim 17, wherein the slider member comprises x-axis biasing members contacting the glass run seal. 19. The vehicle flush window system of claim 11, wherein the slider member and the glass run seal face the interior surface of the door window but are confined not to extend beyond an edge of the door window when the interior surface of the door window is secured to the slider member and the at least part of the slider member is slidingly received in the guide channel. 20. A vehicle flush window system secured to a door including a door window having an exterior surface and an opposite interior surface, the vehicle flush window system comprising: a slider member secured to the interior surface of the door window; and a guiding structure mounted on the vehicle door, the guiding structure comprising a receiving bracket having a guide channel slidingly receiving at least part of the slider member, a glass run seal at least a portion of which is interposed between the receiving bracket and the slider member, and a capping carrier securing the glass run seal in contact with the slider member; and a cover secured by the capping carrier in flush relation to the exterior surface of the door window.
